summary: Full backend + frontend testing for Startup Lead Finder MVP. All 10 backend pytest cases passed (health, create + validation 422s, list ordering, case-insensitive search, delete + 404, stats, CSV export). Frontend e2e (add lead via dialog, debounced search, clear search, delete with confirm dialog + toast, CSV endpoint) all work. One responsive issue found: header overflows at mobile 390px viewport (Add Lead button gets clipped).

issues: Horizontal overflow: document scrollWidth=439px vs viewport=390px. The Header's right-side button group (Export CSV + Add Lead with data-testid=add-company-btn) extends past the right viewport edge, causing the Add Lead button to be partially clipped. Recommend either stacking buttons, shrinking icon-only on small screens, or wrapping the header buttons with flex-wrap and reducing horizontal padding on mobile.

action_items: Fix mobile header overflow at <=390px breakpoint so Add Lead / Export CSV buttons remain fully visible (or wrap below the brand).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event hooks; migrate to lifespan handlers for FastAPI >=0.95 best practice (non-blocking, but will warn in newer versions).
routes/companies.py: `import re` placed inside the request handler — works, but moving to module-level is cleaner.
stats endpoint uses two count_documents queries; for very small data it is fine, but consider a single aggregation for scale.

Models: `website` is typed as Optional[str] (not HttpUrl) — acceptable for an MVP that accepts bare domains, but no URL validation occurs (e.g., the frontend stores 'acme.com' unchanged). Document this or add a light validator.
AddCompanyDialog: when API returns 422 with a list, only detail[0].msg is shown — for multi-field errors users see only one. Consider joining messages.
test_credentials: N/A - open access app, no auth
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up via TEST_ prefix in module-scoped session fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_companies_api.py and clean their TEST_ prefixed rows in teardown. The only outstanding issue is mobile (390px) header overflow — verify after main agent applies a responsive fix by checking document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= window.innerWidth at viewport 390x844.
rca of the issue: Mobile header overflow RCA: /app/frontend/src/components/lead/Header.jsx (not viewed but inferred from LeadFinder.jsx using <Header> with two child Buttons) places the brand + Export CSV (h-10 px-4) + Add Lead (h-10 px-5) in a single row inside a `flex items-center gap-3` container, which on a 390px viewport sums to ~439px and overflows. Mitigation: either (a) use flex-wrap with justify-end so buttons wrap below the brand on small screens, (b) hide button labels on small screens and show only icons, or (c) reduce horizontal padding for mobile (e.g., `px-3 md:px-5`).
